key_insights.insight: A 'public health approach' to crime involves dealing with the immediate infection through policing but also preventing the infection in the first place by addressing underlying issues like deprivation and inequality.
key_insights.quote: Think of crime as you would a public health issue... You deal with the infection, you've got to kill the infection, right? ...But stop the infection occurring in the first place. Stop the crime occurring in the first place.

key_insights.insight: Being a politician, especially in leadership roles, demands balancing emotional responses with rational decision-making, especially during crisis.
key_insights.quote: In real time, there really can't be [emotion] ...because people are looking to you to provide leadership and panic doesn't isn't good leadership and so one of the reasons why you know i've asked for and we've had the practice the preparation the planning is to make sure there isn't panic and there isn't emotion because you've got to make rational decisions and provide reassurance in a cool calm way

key_insights.insight: The impact of Islamophobia and racism can significantly impact an individual's personal safety and also their career. It can also have an effect on the people around them, like staff and family.
key_insights.quote: So we're in this ridiculous situation where because of the hatred against me, people are writing letters and emails to City Hall staff who in the previous 16 years haven't had this... we'vegot a duty of care to our staff right our staff are traumatized upset all the rest of it

key_insights.insight: The responsibility that a politician has is to balance emotion with education and offer more depth and explanations to the complexity of issues.
key_insights.quote: One of the failures of politicians, and I also plead guilty, is we've lost the art to be good teachers, right? Political education is lacking in our country. And so I think a good politician should use his or her role in an unpatronizing way to educate people.

key_insights.insight: Recognizing and addressing systemic cultural issues within institutions, like the police, requires external review and willingness to implement uncomfortable changes.
key_insights.quote: You can't mark your own homework. You need somebody else to look into things, tell you how bad things are, make recommendations and follow the truth.

key_insights.insight: Mentorship plays a crucial role in the development of young people.
key_insights.quote: A lot of young people haven't got that role model at home. The youth worker is that role model. A youth worker is an amazing asset to a young person if you've not got the role model at home.

key_insights.insight: It's essential to share your work life with your family and involve them in your journey to have a strong support system.
key_insights.quote: You've got to make sure that parts of your work life are shared with your home life.

key_insights.insight: Being around people and engaging in social activities are important for maintaining mental well-being, especially in leadership roles.
key_insights.quote: I now realize I thrive on working with people, being around people, on company... I realized I can't work from home in perpetuity. I need to be around people. I need that buzz

actionable_advice.how_to: Share your diary with your spouse, invite them to events, and create shared activities with your kids to share what you're doing.

actionable_advice.advice: Address safety at the source through education
actionable_advice.how_to: Get involved in schools to explain the dangers, and also to ensure the police have enough money for policing in the youth.

actionable_advice.advice: Be honest and transparent with your team
actionable_advice.how_to: Share your work and your struggles with your team.

memorable_stories: Khan's recounts the Grenfell Tower fire, the horrific images, the heat, and the sheer human toll, including one family of six wiped out and an 11-year-old child who had won an essay competition.
Khan describes how his dad was mistreated in his bus garage, felt helpless. Was the motivation for going into law to help people who otherwise wouldn't be able to challenge unfair situations.
Khan's recollection of Donald Trump's attacks after the London Bridge terror attack and what it meant and what it signified.

Khan talks about the racism and homophobia he experienced when the Christchurch shooter and Finsbury Park terrorist referenced him. Also what it was like to have mainstream journalists use him as clickbait.
Khan talks about working all the time but now being more open to spending more time with his friends and his family.
Khan tells of how he's had police protection to protect himself and his family, but at the same time how it makes him less spontaneous.
contrarian_claims: Trump did something well by being authentic in sharing his opinions.
Most MPs have got to be inauthentic because in Parliament, there's something called collective responsibility.
That you should judge somebody by their enemies, not by their friends.
I don't want to win an election because it's time for change by itself. I want you to be inspired and enthused to vote Labour because of our policy offer.
The claim that emotions is important in politics
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Sadiq Khan: Why Are Police Officers Attacking Women? How Safe Are We REALLY?
